candidate_summary: GADA-ROTATE: 5-class rotating-power multi-agent LLM governance with K-turn rotation cycle, 11-grade within-class skill ladder, Salgan-9 consensus transition ratification, and three-principle constraints (term-limit + balanced-opposition + power-sharing), inspired by Ethiopian Oromo Gada indigenous governance.

results.name: ALMC Adaptive LLM-Based Multi-Agent Coordination OpenReview
results.description: Adaptive multi-agent LLM coordination with role-based + periodic switching policy.
results.rationale: ALMC's role-based + periodic switching is functionally the same artefact (role-rotation among specialized agents) and same effect (cyclic authority across roles in LLM multi-agent system). Maps directly onto GADA-ROTATE's 5-class rotation cycle.

results.name: AgentNet Decentralized Evolutionary Coordination (2504.00587)
results.description: Dynamic specialization + adjustable connectivity + task routing without predefined workflows.
results.rationale: AgentNet has dynamic specialization but lacks the explicit class-rotation cycle, term-limits, and consensus transitions. Different artefact: decentralized self-organizing vs. fixed 5-class rotation. Same broad paradigm space but distinct mechanism.

results.name: PoliSim CHI 2026 LLM Agent Simulation Policy
results.description: LLM agent simulation for policy with role rotation.
results.rationale: PoliSim explicitly does role rotation for policy simulation — same artefact (rotating roles in LLM agents) and same effect (governance simulation). Strong functional match on rotation mechanism even if Salgan-9 specific is absent.

results.name: LLM Collaboration Multi-Agent RL (2508.04652)
results.description: MARL collaboration framework with rotating role assignment + cooperative training.
results.rationale: MARL with rotating role assignment + cooperative training is the same artefact (rotating roles among specialized agents) and same effect (cyclic role assumption with coordination). Strong functional twin to 5-class K-turn rotation.

results.name: RL LLM Multi-Agent Orchestration Traces (2605.02801)
results.description: Orchestration traces with agent-, role-, turn-, orchestrator-level credit signals + token-to-team rotation.
results.rationale: Has rotation + role/turn-level credit but focuses on RL credit assignment rather than governance rotation. Related paradigm but the artefact (RL credit signals) differs from GADA's governance term-limit rotation.

results.name: Multi-Agent Orchestration 2026 Codebridge
results.description: Puppeteer orchestrators + specialist agents + role-based design patterns.
results.rationale: Static orchestrator + specialist topology — no rotation cycle, no term-limit, no consensus delegate transition. Different artefact (fixed hierarchy vs rotating power).

results.name: LLM Multi-Agent Software Engineering Survey (2404.04834)
results.description: Comprehensive survey of LLM-MAS — role-based specialization + orchestration.
results.rationale: Survey covers role-based specialization broadly but does not propose rotation cycle, 11-grade ladder, or Salgan consensus. Survey-level coverage rather than same-artefact mechanism.

results.name: Multi-Agent LLM Systems Preprint Nov 2025
results.description: Multi-agent LLM with rotating-specialization governance + power-sharing.
results.rationale: EXACT TWIN. Explicitly describes rotating-specialization governance + power-sharing — same artefact (rotating-specialization multi-agent governance) and same effect (power-sharing through rotation). Strong overlap on multiple content words.

decision_rationale: Independent verification: 4 out of 8 results are hits (IDs 1, 3, 4, 8). Result 8 (Multi-Agent LLM Preprint Nov 2025) is an explicit rotating-specialization governance + power-sharing twin with 4 keyword overlaps and high semantic/functional scores. Results 1 (ALMC role-based + periodic switching), 3 (PoliSim role rotation), and 4 (MARL rotating role assignment) all functionally match the core 5-class rotation mechanism at >=0.7 functional score. The novelty claim of K-turn class-rotation + power-sharing governance is saturated in prior art — recombination of well-known multi-agent rotation paradigms. Hit count >=1 triggers FAIL.
